task_completion.description: LibreOffice Calc is open and displays the initial dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet window is visible showing columns 'Name' and 'Score' with the initial unsorted data (Alice, Bob, Charlie, David, Eve).

task_completion.description: The dataset is selected for sorting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Cells containing the data (e.g., A1 to B6 or A2 to B6) are highlighted, indicating they are selected.

task_completion.description: The agent initiates the sorting process.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Data' menu is open with 'Sort...' highlighted, OR the mouse cursor is clicking the 'Sort Ascending' button on the toolbar.

task_completion.description: The agent configures the sort criteria or executes the sort.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sort dialog box is open showing 'Score' (or Column B) as the Sort Key and 'Ascending' selected, OR the spreadsheet updates to show the data being rearranged.

task_completion.description: The dataset is correctly sorted by Score in ascending order.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet rows are ordered exactly as: David (63), Bob (72), Alice (85), Eve (88), Charlie (95).

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreOffice Calc GUI to perform the sort.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the LibreOffice Calc application menus, dialogs, or toolbars, rather than a terminal window or script editor.

integrity.description: The agent used the built-in sort functionality rather than manually retyping the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no text cursor active in the cells editing the names or scores manually; the sorting happens via dialogs or toolbar buttons.

integrity.description: All original data is preserved without loss or corruption.
integrity.visual_evidence: All 5 original students and their exact scores are present in the final result, and the Name-Score pairs match the original data.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 5 students: Alice (85), Bob (72), Charlie (95), David (63), Eve (88). The expected sorted order by Score (ascending) is: 1. David (63), 2. Bob (72), 3. Alice (85), 4. Eve (88), 5. Charlie (95). Success requires the Score column to be sorted ascending, Name-Score pairs to be maintained, and all data preserved.
